_meta.description: Static metadata per market. Volatile fields (MW, $/kWh, vacancy, pipeline, facilities) come live from /api/v1/*.

northern-virginia.name: Northern Virginia
northern-virginia.description: Northern Virginia's Ashburn corridor is the undisputed global capital of data centers with 3+ GW operational and 9.6 GW pipeline. Ground zero for cloud, hyperscale AI, and enterprise infrastructure.

dallas.name: Dallas-Fort Worth
dallas.description: One of the fastest-growing US markets with ERCOT grid independence, no state income tax, and 4 GW planned pipeline. Hyperscale magnet across Garland, Allen, and Plano.

phoenix.name: Phoenix
phoenix.description: Rocketed to #2 globally with 4.7 GW planned. Abundant solar, low disaster risk, massive land availability. Meta, Google, Microsoft expanding across Mesa, Goodyear, Glendale.

chicago.name: Chicago
chicago.description: Nation's #3 market. Midwest connectivity hub with CME/CBOE trading, Elk Grove Village expansion, and Prime Data Centers' $1B investment fueling momentum.

atlanta.name: Atlanta
atlanta.description: Tripled capacity to 1,279 MW in one year. Hyperscale, AI startup, and enterprise demand. Could rival Dallas by 2030.

silicon-valley.name: Silicon Valley
silicon-valley.description: Heart of tech with unmatched connectivity. 10 MW+ deals saw 19% pricing increase. Severe power constraints but premium value.

new-york.name: New York / New Jersey
new-york.description: Wall Street's backbone. 60 Hudson Street connectivity. NJ corridors growing with hyperscale. Densest US population center.

seattle.name: Seattle / Pacific Northwest
seattle.tagline: Hydropower Hub — Cheapest Power in NA
seattle.description: Cheapest power in North America: $0.02-0.04/kWh hydro. Microsoft & Amazon HQ. 95%+ renewable. Central WA 100% renewable operations.

los-angeles.name: Los Angeles
los-angeles.description: Hollywood's digital backbone. One Wilshire premier carrier hotel. Streaming, gaming, AI content driving demand.

denver.name: Denver
denver.description: Low disaster risk, cool climate, central positioning. Renewable energy and edge computing demand growing.

austin.name: Austin-San Antonio
austin.description: Texas tech capital + San Antonio companion market. ERCOT, no state tax, booming AI startup ecosystem.

portland.name: Portland / Oregon
portland.description: 90%+ renewable hydro, no sales tax, Hillsboro Silicon Forest. Eastern Oregon emerging for hyperscale.

columbus.name: Columbus, OH
columbus.description: 3.3 GW planned. Intel $20B, Cologix 800 MW AI campus, Meta/Amazon. Within 750 miles of half the US.

reno.name: Reno, NV
reno.description: 3.4 GW planned pipeline. Nevada tax advantages, California adjacency without regulations. Twin megaprojects underway.

salt-lake-city.name: Salt Lake City
salt-lake-city.description: Meta Eagle Mountain megacampus. 2M+ sq ft growth. Affordable land, renewables, strategic Western position.

richmond.name: Richmond, VA
richmond.description: Key East Coast alternative. NoVA spillover along I-95. Subsea cable proximity. 2.5M sq ft developed.

las-vegas.name: Las Vegas
las-vegas.description: Switch's iconic SUPERNAP. No state income tax, abundant solar, gaming industry presence.

raleigh-durham.name: Raleigh-Durham
raleigh-durham.description: World-class university talent, favorable climate, Duke Energy grid upgrades. Attractive SE emerging market.

nashville.name: Nashville, TN
nashville.description: Central location, expanding tech, healthcare IT capital driving enterprise DC demand.

minneapolis.name: Minneapolis
minneapolis.description: Strategic edge positioning. Cool climate reduces cooling. Enterprise & healthcare demand.

des-moines.name: Des Moines, IA
des-moines.description: Rapid expansion: Google, Meta campuses. Wind power, affordable land, low risk, strong incentives.

kansas-city.name: Kansas City
kansas-city.description: Geographic center of the US. Strong fiber. Tertiary market benefiting from heartland expansion.

omaha.name: Omaha, NE
omaha.description: Adding 2.2M sq ft. Google campus. Cheap power, fiber, central location.

pittsburgh.name: Pittsburgh, PA
pittsburgh.description: Carnegie Mellon AI/robotics. Revitalized industrial spaces. Strong AI ecosystem.

jackson-ms.name: Jackson, MS
jackson-ms.description: Amazon $10B for two DCs. Duke Energy 2.24 GW complex. Major tax exemptions.

montreal.name: Montréal
montreal.description: Cheapest power in NA via Hydro-Québec ($0.03/kWh). Cool climate, Mila AI institute, bilingual tech talent.

toronto.name: Toronto
toronto.description: Canada's largest & most mature market. Financial hub. Strong banking sector demand.

london.name: London
london.description: Europe's largest: 1+ GW operational, 1.3 GW planned. Slough, Docklands, West London. EMEA financial capital.

frankfurt.name: Frankfurt
frankfurt.description: DE-CIX world's largest IX. 13.7% inventory growth. 1.1 GW planned (47% preleased). Germany's digital backbone.

amsterdam.name: Amsterdam
amsterdam.description: Major IX despite 2020 moratorium headwinds. 18% pricing increase, zero new supply. Shifting to Rotterdam, Almere.

paris.name: Paris
paris.description: Surpassed Amsterdam as EU #3. Sovereign cloud + AI driving 11.2% growth. Vacancy: 16.1%→7.7%.

dublin.name: Dublin
dublin.description: Crossed 1 GW. DCs expected 32% of Ireland's electricity by 2026. Critical US tech hub.

madrid.name: Madrid
madrid.description: Spain emerging. 300 MW near Pamplona. Strong renewables, EU data sovereignty demand.

milan.name: Milan
milan.description: Joining FLAPD elite. 69% of 656 MW preleased — highest in Europe. Southern Europe powerhouse.

brussels.name: Brussels
brussels.description: Scalable-power alternative to Amsterdam. Kevlinx wholesale project. EU institutional demand.

warsaw.name: Warsaw
warsaw.description: Central & Eastern Europe leader. Bridges West-East. EU digital transformation.

zurich.name: Zürich
zurich.description: Swiss privacy laws + finance demand. Premium market. 100% renewable hydro grid.

stockholm.name: Stockholm
stockholm.description: 100% renewable grid. Cool climate. Brookfield, EcoDataCenter expanding. Europe's sustainability leader.

oslo.name: Oslo
oslo.description: 98%+ renewable hydro. Lowest EU energy costs. Cool climate. Growing international interest.

helsinki.name: Helsinki
helsinki.description: Waste heat reuse pioneer. Cool climate. Nordic enterprise and government demand.

tokyo.name: Tokyo
tokyo.description: APAC largest with 1.6 GW planned (35% committed). AI/HPC fueling suburban wholesale expansion. $190-355/kW.

singapore.name: Singapore
singapore.description: $310-470/kW — APAC's most expensive. Tight capacity post-moratorium. Spillover to Johor and Batam.

hong-kong.name: Hong Kong
hong-kong.description: Asia's financial hub. 28% vacancy = competitive pricing. Chinese customer dynamics.

seoul.name: Seoul
seoul.description: Samsung, SK, Korean cloud providers. World's strongest semiconductor ecosystem. Massive 5G investment.

sydney.name: Sydney
sydney.description: Amazon $13B investment. 700-900 MW planned. Melbourne emerging secondary.

melbourne.name: Melbourne
melbourne.description: Sydney alternative. 700-900 MW planned. Amazon $13B boosting both cities.

mumbai.name: Mumbai
mumbai.description: #6 globally under construction: 335 MW building (+62%). India doubling to 1,800 MW by 2026.

chennai.name: Chennai
chennai.description: India #2 market. Major submarine cable landing hub. IT services sector growth.

bangkok.name: Bangkok
bangkok.description: Digital Edge + B.Grimm $1B campuses. Thailand as SE Asian hub. Favorable energy costs.

jakarta.name: Jakarta
jakarta.description: Projected $3B by 2026 (12.95% CAGR). Tier 4 facility approved. Batam emerging.

johor.name: Johor, Malaysia
johor.description: Singapore overflow. 82% preleased. Microsoft $2B, DayOne $3.5B green DC.

taipei.name: Taipei
taipei.description: TSMC home. Chip-DC synergies. Hyperscaler cloud regions. Submarine cable hub.

dubai.name: Dubai
dubai.description: ME premier hub connecting EU-Asia. Free zone incentives. Sovereign cloud push.

riyadh.name: Riyadh
riyadh.description: Vision 2030 investment. Oracle, Google, AWS cloud regions. Ultra-cheap energy. Renewables transition.

sao-paulo.name: São Paulo
sao-paulo.description: 67% of LATAM inventory. Equinix, Ascenty, ODATA expanding. 13% CAGR. Abundant renewables.

santiago.name: Santiago
santiago.description: Chile's stable economy, renewables, near-record-low vacancy. Growing cloud demand.

queretaro.name: Querétaro
queretaro.description: Less than 1 MW available — tightest in LATAM. Rising prices from power constraints. New fiber routes.

johannesburg.name: Johannesburg
johannesburg.description: Africa's most established market. AWS, MSFT, Google live. Subsea cable investment transforming connectivity.

nairobi.name: Nairobi
nairobi.description: East Africa digital hub. M-Pesa mobile-first economy. Subsea cable growth. Enterprise demand.
